name: Saket Mundhada
tagline: Full-stack AI automation engineer — I turn natural-language goals into shipped agent workflows.
summary: Full-stack AI automation engineer who scopes messy business workflows, builds LLM-powered prototypes, and turns working patterns into reusable agent runtimes. Built WebGPT, an open-source browser/spreadsheet agent system with an MCP-style connector model; deployed WebGPT-powered workflows for a real estate operations team; and prototyped DesignGPT, an embeddable enterprise AI-agent framework for Lutron web applications. Focused on Forward Deployed and Applied AI engineering — embedding with customers and shipping agents into production.
lookingFor: Forward Deployed Engineer / Applied AI roles — embedding with customers and shipping agents into production.

projects.title: WebGPT — Open-Source AI Workflow Agent Platform
projects.stack: TypeScript, React, Chrome MV3, Node.js, Express, OpenAI APIs, OpenAPI, Google Sheets API, Microsoft Graph
projects.summary: An open-source Chrome runtime for AI browser agents that turns plain-English goals into executed work across the browser, Google Sheets, and Excel — built around a clean runtime/planner boundary and a connector model. Released to beta users and under active development.
bullets: Published a Chrome MV3 extension with a deployed hosted planner backend that turns natural-language goals into executed workflows across the browser, Google Sheets, and Excel.
Designed a connector model — MCP-server-style — where site adapters and API-backed 'surfaces' (Google Sheets, Microsoft Excel) are different flavors of connectors the agent plugs into.
Kept a crisp runtime/planner boundary: the extension owns browser execution and state extraction; any backend that speaks the documented OpenAPI contract can own planning.
Built the Chrome runtime — sidepanel UX, structured multi-frame state extraction, action execution, navigation recovery, human-in-the-loop confirmation, and replay.
Turned successful runs into reusable routines (templates) that re-run across new inputs instead of starting from zero every time.
Released to beta users and under active development — roadmap includes a user-facing skill to create a connector for any site (DOM or API), with OAuth and PKCE handled for you.

projects.title: WebGPT for CAs — GST Reconciliation Workflow (Sachdev Associates)
projects.stack: WebGPT, Google Sheets, connector-style tool actions, accounting-domain rules
projects.summary: A CA-focused GST automation vertical built on WebGPT for Sachdev Associates, a Mumbai chartered-accountancy firm — turning repetitive GST reconciliation into structured, reviewable agent workflows.
bullets: Built a CA-focused GST automation extension for WebGPT with Sachdev Associates, a Mumbai chartered-accountancy firm, performing invoice-level reconciliation between the GST portal register (GSTR-2B) and client purchase registers — separating matched, missing, portal-only, and close-match entries into CA-reviewable tabs, with a workflow designed to extend to credit/debit notes, ITC, ISD credits, and imports.

Designed domain-aware agent workflows that detect GST filing intent, read structured spreadsheet data, apply deterministic reconciliation logic, and write CA-reviewable output tabs with matched invoices, close matches, missing ITC, mismatches, and filing-impact summaries.

Implemented WebGPT's GST engine as a modular vertical workflow layer — combining browser/sheet automation, connector-style tool actions, workflow hints, and accounting-domain rules — so the agent executes specialized CA workflows while preserving the general WebGPT platform.

Positioned WebGPT as an AI back-office platform for chartered accountants: turning repetitive GST compliance into structured agent workflows that cut manual spreadsheet review, surface filing risks, and speed client GST return prep.

projects.title: DesignGPT — Embeddable Enterprise AI-Agent Framework (Lutron)
projects.stack: TypeScript, Node, React, embeddable agent runtime, WebGPT engine pattern
projects.summary: WebGPT's engine packaged as a Node module so Lutron web applications can expose their own domain skills while the framework handles chat, planning, and execution.
bullets: Built a prototype embeddable AI-agent framework — WebGPT's engine as a Node module — so host apps can drop in an agent without rebuilding planning or execution.
Host applications expose their own domain skills; the shared framework handles chat, planning, and the execution loop.
Reuses WebGPT's tool/action contract and runtime boundary, bringing the same intent-to-action engine inside an enterprise product surface.

projects.title: POC Biotech — Pre-Sales Ordering Tool + Admin AI Inventory Assistant (Client Deployment)
projects.stack: Node.js, Express, OpenAI tool-calling, SQLite, Railway, Resend
projects.summary: A pre-sales ordering website for a Mumbai pharmaceutical distributor — customers submit product requests — with an admin-gated AI assistant that helps the owners with inventory and reordering.
bullets: Built and deployed a pre-sales ordering website where the distributor's customers browse the catalog and submit product requests.
Routed incoming quote requests to the owners by email (via Resend), replacing manual back-and-forth with a structured pipeline.

Added an admin-gated AI inventory assistant (OpenAI tool-calling) that helps the owners decide what to reorder — answering natural-language questions over their ERP data: stock on hand, expiry, reorder suggestions, and slow movers.
Grounded every answer in real data with read-only analytics tools and guarded, SELECT-only SQL over normalized purchase/sale transactions.
Reduced purchasing ~3% by forecasting reorders against sitting inventory; scoped end to end with the owner and shipped as a single Railway service.

projects.title: Student Landing — Real Estate Operations Automation
projects.stack: WebGPT, Dotloop connector, Atlas Philly, Philadelphia government websites
projects.summary: Real forward-deployed work: embedded with a Philadelphia real-estate firm's operations team and deployed WebGPT routines for document prep and property verification.
bullets: Embedded with Student Landing's operations team and deployed WebGPT routines that automate Dotloop agreement prep and property verification against city and third-party sites.
Built WebGPT's Dotloop connector, which drives Dotloop through API calls rather than brittle DOM steps — now being promoted to a first-class 'surface' (like Google Sheets) for speed.
Hardened field mapping, source-value extraction, replay, and human-review patterns through real operator use under deadline pressure.

projects.title: CropGPT — Intent-to-Edit Media Automation Engine
projects.stack: Python, FFmpeg, OpenAI APIs, image-generation models, JSON Schema
projects.summary: A creative workflow engine that translates intent into reusable edit actions — and into generative interior-design specs.
bullets: Translates creative intent into reusable edit actions: cropping, captioning, CTA placement, audio normalization, export, and QA.
Produced WebGPT's YouTube Shorts demos from raw recordings — vertical cuts with branded captions, transcripts, and review notes.
Extended into generative interior-design workflows using OpenAI APIs and image-generation models: rough room plans and style intent become furnished-room image specs.
Recipe-driven pipeline with validation, deterministic FFmpeg rendering, and loudness checks, so new formats define their own editing actions.

projects.title: SOFR Forecasting & Futures Pricing Model
projects.stack: Python, Arbitrage-Free Nelson-Siegel, particle filtering, CME SOFR futures
projects.summary: A SOFR rate forecasting model that estimates the latent term-structure state and prices futures for spread-trading signals.
bullets: Built a SOFR rate forecasting model on the Arbitrage-Free Nelson-Siegel framework in Python.
Implemented a particle filter (Sequential Importance Resampling) instead of a Kalman filter to estimate latent state variables — level, slope, and curvature — from historical SOFR data.
Calibrated on daily settlement SOFR rates and CME SOFR futures prices, predicting futures at 5-minute intervals.
Identified mean-reversion levels to signal entry points for spread-trading strategies (flies, strips, packs, bundles).

projects.title: Mean-Reverting Portfolio Optimization
projects.stack: Python, CVXPY, SPASM, scikit-learn, SDP / Sparse PCA
projects.summary: An engine that searches for synthetic mean-reverting portfolios via convex optimization and backtests them.
bullets: Built a Python engine to search for synthetic mean-reverting portfolios by minimizing predictability, Portmanteau, and crossing statistics with SDP relaxations.
Solved the convex optimization via Sparse PCA using CVXPY, SPASM, and scikit-learn.
Backtested 150 options (2015–2020) with Jurek & Yang's strategy across the top 50 portfolios, achieving Sharpe ratios of 1.8–2.3.
Reduced transaction costs 20–35% by designing sparse portfolios that minimized the number of assets traded.

projects.title: Interactive Brokers Trading Bot
projects.stack: Python, Interactive Brokers API, Matplotlib, Plotly
projects.summary: A variance-minimization trading bot that diversifies risk across S&P 500 sectors with monthly rebalancing.
bullets: Designed a portfolio variance-minimization trading bot via the Interactive Brokers API, diversifying across 11 S&P 500 sectors with monthly rebalancing.
Achieved annualized volatility around 6.3% and annualized risk-adjusted returns around 5%.
Built dynamic dashboards (Matplotlib, Plotly) tracking sector allocations, returns, and volatility.

projects.title: Figure-Extraction Deep-Learning Pipeline
projects.stack: Python, deep learning, arXiv dataset, Hungarian algorithm
projects.summary: A distantly-supervised pipeline that extracts and indexes tabular figures from hundreds of thousands of scanned research papers.
bullets: Built a distantly-supervised neural-network pipeline to extract and index tabular figures from 200k+ scanned research papers.
Auto-generated high-quality training labels from arXiv metadata instead of hand-labeling.
Evaluated bounding-box accuracy by matching predictions to ground truth via the Hungarian algorithm.

projects.title: Work-Stealing Thread Pool & Fork-Join Framework (C++)
projects.stack: C++, lock-free queues, atomics, OS threading
projects.summary: A C++ fork-join runtime for dynamic task parallelism, optimized for low synchronization overhead.
bullets: Built a work-stealing thread pool and fork-join runtime for divide-and-conquer parallelism.
Minimized per-task synchronization overhead using Dijkstra's mutual-exclusion algorithm over lock-based deques.
Tested scalability and robustness with up to 50 worker threads and input sizes from n = 20 to 200k.

projects.title: Probabilistic DBMS Index Using Skip Lists
projects.summary: A DBMS indexing structure using skip lists for expected O(log n) operations, benchmarked against tree-based indices.
bullets: Implemented a DBMS index using skip lists for expected O(log n) search, insertion, and deletion.
Used probabilistic balancing to promote frequently-accessed elements.
Evaluated performance tradeoffs against tree-based indices.

experience.role: Project Application Software Engineer, promoted to Senior (Dec 2023)
bullets: Built an internal AI design-assistant chatbot on AWS Bedrock, indexing Lutron's UX-principles docs (RAG) to give designers and product managers context-aware guidance and rapid validation of new UI proposals.

Built DesignGPT, a prototype embeddable AI-agent framework — WebGPT's engine as a Node module — so Lutron web applications could expose their own domain skills while the framework handled chat, planning, and execution.

Designed distributed backend services in Go exposing standardized APIs for rule evaluation and asset generation, including a fault-tolerant image-generation pipeline (Go Lambdas, SQS, API Gateway, S3) with a decoupled producer/consumer design.

Architected Bill-of-Materials services (hierarchical grouping, batch operations, rollback, deterministic state sync) and a MuleSoft–Go integration mapping product models to Salesforce RLM for an ERP migration.

Built a React + TypeScript micro-frontend (container-presenter, Zustand) with real-time 3D product visualization (Threekit), cutting design rework ~40%; added Cypress visual-regression gates to CI/CD.
Resolved 100+ production defects and multiple P1/P2 outages during a multi-system ERP transition, protecting revenue-critical quoting and ordering workflows.
Shipped an Azure C# notification service automating high-volume quote and order communications, with retry logic, telemetry, and SLA-aligned delivery.
Integrated a React micro-frontend into a WPF desktop application (WebView, service workers, offline caching) for a hybrid desktop–web experience with local fallback.
Built a REST-to-LEAP translation layer for real-time control of Lutron devices — lighting, scenes, and shades — with command serialization and state validation.
Migrated Mississippi's state healthcare-support site to AWS and refactored it to Java Spring and Angular under HIPAA; shipped WebSocket APIs for real-time updates and REST APIs for CRUD and search.
